name: BM 41462
title: Astronomical Diary: Halley's Comet
description: Babylonian astronomical diary recording the appearance of Halley's Comet in September–October 164 BCE, the earliest confirmed written record of this comet. The text notes the comet's path relative to the Pleiades and observations of planetary positions.
raw_atf:
&BM41462 = BM 41462 (Babylonian Astronomical Diary, 164 BCE)
#atf: lang akk
#note: Seleucid period astronomical diary. British Museum. Records Halley's Comet, September–October 164 BCE.

sections.content: #note: Seleucid period astronomical diary. British Museum. Records Halley's Comet, September–October 164 BCE.

sections.translation: Year 147 of the Seleucid Era, month Ululu.
Day 1 through day 30:
a hairy star (comet) appeared before the Pleiades,
moving toward the south.
Day 15: Jupiter was in Virgo.
It passed through the rear of Leo.
Its tail (coma) pointed south.

sections.content: UD 20.KAM MUL BABBAR ina ABSIN KI u4-me AN-BAR4
sections.translation: Day 20: Jupiter in Virgo in the daytime sky.

sections.content: sza3 GE6 AN-BAR4 ina EN.NUN AN-BAR4
sections.translation: During the night of the south wind
in the morning watch of the south wind

sections.content: MUL BABBAR ina 3 KUS3 NIM AN-BAR4
sections.translation: in Virgo, elevated 2 cubits,
Jupiter 3 cubits elevated southward.
A great eclipse of the god
was moving toward Jupiter.
The Pleiades were above Virgo, moving.
Day 21: in the night of the south wind

sections.content: ina EN.NUN AN-BAR4 DELE-bat ina 1 KUS3
sections.translation: in the morning watch, Venus was 1 cubit

sections.content: ina IGI MAS.MAS GE6 6 ina si-man
sections.translation: in front of Gemini. Night of the 6th, in the evening,
an eclipse of the great god, Venus,
was moving before him — I observed it.
I observed (it) before him in the south wind,
moving toward the south.

sections.content: MUL BABBAR ina ABSIN ina GE6 1 ina si-man
sections.translation: Jupiter in Virgo, on the 1st night, in the evening,
a great eclipse of the great goddess
appeared in the midst of the false crown.
